title: Oreocharis trichantha
intro: Oreocharis trichantha is a plant species belonging to the Gesneriaceae family, commonly referred to as a type of flowering plant. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Oreocharis trichantha include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Oreocharis trichantha is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions to alleviate symptoms associated with fever, cough, and respiratory distress.

The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems. Its roots and leaves are the primary medicinal parts used in treatments. The roots are used to treat fever and rheumatism due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
